Day Trip to Chefchaouen From Tangier

Day Trip to Chefchaouen From Tangier. Embark on a captivating journey from Tangier to the enchanting blue city of Chefchaouen. As your adventure begins, Xauen Tours expert guides will lead you through the labyrinth of its charming streets, where you’ll encounter the iconic blue doors and vibrant stairs that define this unique Moroccan gem.

But this tour is more than just a visual delight. Dive into the rich history, culture, and lifestyle of Chefchaouen, as our knowledgeable guides provide fascinating insights that breathe life into every corner of this captivating destination. It’s an experience that will immerse you in the heart of this extraordinary place, leaving you with unforgettable memories of Chefchaouen’s undeniable allure.

Join a local guide on a day trip from Tangier to Chefchaouen with hotel pickup and drop-off included. Explore the Kasbah of Chefchaouen, the main square, the central mosque, and more while learning about the city’s history.

Starting from Tangier at 9:00 AM, travel high up into the Rif Mountains to discover Chefchaouen, the beautiful blue door and white-walled Medina, which was originally a Berber post.

Enjoy a guided tour of the city when you arrive. See the main square, the heart of Chefchaouen, with its 18th-century kasbah, central mosque, and cafes with authentic Moroccan-style menus. Stroll the market to discover a mix of traditional Moroccan and tourist souvenirs.

Visit the Jewish Quarter behind the jewelry souk to discover a unique part of the city. Drive back to Tangier and arrive by 7:00 PM.

Highlights
Visit the beautiful old town of Chefchaouen
Discover the history and the culture of Chefchaouen
Learn about its Arabo-Andalusian history from a local guide

Best Private Tangier  Day Trip

Welcome to an extraordinary Tangier Day Trip, exclusively crafted for you by Xauen Tours. Our meticulously designed excursion promises an unforgettable journey through the captivating city of Tangier. With us, you’ll discover the hidden gems, rich history, and vibrant culture of this Moroccan gem. Get ready to embark on an adventure that will leave you with cherished memories and a deeper connection to the enchanting city of Tangier.

The guided tour of Tangier consists of a sightseeing tour around Tangier covering the most popular landmarks including the Ville Nouvelle, the Cap Spartel & the Hercules Caves. The walking tour of the Kasbah and the Medina for the purpose of learning about the local culture. This private tour offers an opportunity to come to terms with the Moroccan lifestyle..

Upon arrival, you’ll be greeted by your tour guide . You’ll start with a sightseeing tour around Tangier with an interesting drive through the Ville Nouvelle. The new town has a rich history as it was built by the eight international powers that ruled Tangier because the city, unlike other Moroccan parts, was appointed as an International Zone. It’s divided into districts with the name of the country that was behind its construction…

You’ll include a short stop by the Mohamed V Mosque, which is the highest Islamic structure in Tangier. Learn about the religion of Islam, and how the city was and still a perfect example of tolerance and peaceful coexitence among all religions. Drive through the California district with its fancy villas and places before reaching the promontory Cap Spartel. It’s at this point where the Mediterranean & the Atlantic Seas meet. The views are stunning with the standing lighthouse that was founded in 1860 by the International Powers to guide the ships.

Continue on the track along the Atlantic with the possibility to include a camel ride experience, then to the Neolithic Caves of Hercules that are shrouded in mystery. The caves are related to the Greek mythology and the opening into the sea is gorgeous.

From Tangier Full-Day Tour Tangier, Asilah, Cap Spartel Tour

With Xauen tours, Embark on a Full Day Tangier Asilah Cape Spartel Tour and join a walking tour of the old city. Visit sites like Hercules Caves and Cape Spartel and enjoy a camel ride with fascinating views of the city..

This tour is a combination of sightseeing and culture. Visit both Cape Spartel and Hercules Caves, also the camel ride to take in panoramic views of the city, and head up to the white jewel of the north.

Discover the Medina, the Kasbah, and Morocco’s beautiful beaches. After a pickup from the airport, port, or your hotel in Tangier, begin the tour around 9:00 AM. The day will be devoted to discovering the beautiful Tangier and Asilah.

Upon arrival in Asilah, embark on a walking tour to discover the Old Medina, medieval ramparts, and the workshops of craftsmen. Experience the life of the locals and begin to understand the history, culture, and lifestyles of the area. Pick up insights throughout the day from your English-, Spanish-, French-, and Arabic-speaking driver.

Head back to Tangier enjoy 2 hours of a leisure time in the old Medina of Tangier with possibility to see the Jewish Quarter and Synagogue, the American legation, and the Jewish Graveyard,to see the Grand Socco, Mendoubia Park, and the market.

Highlights
Discover the Hercules Caves, known for its mythological history
Marvel at Cape Spartel, the entrance to the Strait of Gibraltar
See where the Mediterranean Sea meets the Atlantic Ocean
Soak in panoramic landscapes of Tangier with a camel ride
Explore a labyrinth of alleyways in the Medina of Tangier

From Tangier: Special Day Trip To Chefchaouen & Tetouan
 

Join us in Day Trip To Chefchaouen & Tetouan With XauenTours and Discover the vibrant town of Chefchaouen in the heart of the Rif Mountains on a day trip from Tangier. Take a guided tour of the town before making a stop in the ancient city of Tetouan.

Explore the smaller towns of Northern Marocco on a day trip from Tangier. Visit Chefchaouen and take a tour of its famous colorful streets. Then, stop off in Tetouan and learn about the city’s Andalusian roots. Admire views of the Rif Mountains on the journey.

Start with pickup from your hotel in Tangier. On the drive to Chefchaouen, which lasts approximately two hours, there’ll be time to stop for a coffee or tea and admire views of the Rif mountain range.

Arrive in Chefchaouen and start exploring the so-called Blue Pearl of Morocco. Take a tour of its iconic medina, featuring traditional architecture and vibrant blue facades. Learn about the town’s history as you visit other sites including the Jewish Cemetery.

Before returning to Tangier, you’ll have time for a stop in Tetouan to explore the ancient city at your own leisure. Visit the medina, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, and learn about the rich history and cultural significance of the town. The tour will end with drop-off at your hotel in Tangier.

Highlights
Immerse yourself in the dazzling blue streets of Chefchaouen on a guided tour
Learn about the fascinating Andalusian roots of Tetouan as you explore the city
Stop to savor a coffee or tea and take in stunning views of the Rif Mountains
Taste the bright flavors of authentic Moroccan food in Chefchaouen’s restaurants
Benefit from convenient round-trip transportation from your hotel in Tangier

Tour guide
Best Rabat day trip  From Tangier

Sign up for the RABAT DAY TRIP from tangier and spend the day exploring what this UNESCO listed and administrative capital of Morocco has to offer. You’ll drive through the highway directly to Rabat and you’ll cover the following highlights:

Oudayas Kasbah
Old Medina
Mohammed V Mausoleum
Hassan Tower
Museum of Archaelogy
Mechouar or Royal Palace
Chellah Necropolis

Your local private tour guide will unveil the mystery of Rabat and make you immerse in the culture. Learn about the Moroccan culture and lifestyle and in the evening, you drive back to Tangier.

Tour Pictures
What to expect

Pick up from Tangier hotel/Riad accommodation in the early morning, head to Rabat through the highway. Upon arrival in Rabat, you’ll meet up with your private local tour guide who was born and grew up in this authentic city, so you are assured to be accompanied by an ambassador of the city.

Meander around the white-washed intricate streets of the Oudayas Kasbah. This district boasts itself with a rich history, founded by the Almoravids in the 11th century, then completely destroyed and rebuilt by the Almohads in the 12th century. The grand Mosque with its unique minaret is eye-catching, the views from here are stunning as it’s built by the everflowing Bou Regreg River overlooking the city of Sale and the Atlantic Ocean. Visit the Andalusian Gardens that were built by the late Sultan Moulay Ismail, also make a visit to the Moulay Ismail Museum.

Stretch your legs in the vibrant and hassle-free Souks of the Medina leading you to the Mohammed V Mausoleum. This unique building represents the Moroccan Architecture with the Royal Guards with whom you can take pictures. Adjacent to the Mausoleum is the Hassan Tower, which is the landmark of Rabat and can be seen from different directions.

Drive to the Mechouar or Royal Palace because Rabat is the administrative capital of Morocco since 1912 and home of the King and the Royal Family. Have a walk by the entrance of the Palace where you can see the guards and the beautiful gardens.

You may want to include a visit to the Archaelogical Museum and the Chellah Necropolis before driving back to Tangier in the late afternoon through the highway.

As always, we’ll make sure to drop you off in your hotel if it’s in the new town; otherwise, we’ll make sure that you are escorted to your Riad within the walls of the Medina or the Kasbah.]

LARACHE DAY TRIP FROM TANGIER
 

Sign up for the private Larache day trip from Tangier & spend the day exploring this less-travelled city that has a rich history and a unique culture. The city is just an hour drive from Tangier by the highway but once you are in the Medina of Chefchaouen, you’ll notice how unspoiled by tourism it’s.

The history of the city goes back to the Carthaginian and the Roman epokes. It served as the major Moroccan port in the 15th century. Moroccan Desert tour 8 days from Casablanca to Tangier 
 

The private Moroccan Desert tour 8 days from Casablanca via Marrakech, High Atlas Mountains, Ait Benhaddou, Skoura, Dades Valley, Merzouga Desert, Fes, Chefchaouen and Tangier offers an ideal opportunity to learn about the deep-seated heritage of Morocco while enjoying the natural potentials that this amazing destination has to offer. We will meet & greet you upon arrival in Casablanca before embarking on a journey of a lifetime experience. You’ll be touring around Morocco with one of our professional private drivers who knows like the back of their hands. In cities like Marrakech, Fes & Chefchaouen you’ll have local tour guides to explore with.

This designed tour Moroccan Desert tour 8 days itinerary remains a sample for you to form an idea but we can customize to meet your timeframe & special requirements.

Day 1: Casablanca — Marrakech

Meet & greet upon arrival in the Mohammed V International Airport, then head to Casablanca’s city center where the dramatic Hassan II Mosque is built by the Atlantic Ocean. It is the third-largest mosque in the Islamic World & is a unique masterpiece of Moroccan & Andalusian Architectures. Have a drive by the Ain Diab paved Corniche before heading to Marrakech through the highway.

Day 2: Full-Day Guided Tour of Marrakech

After having breakfast in your hotel, you will have a cultural guided tour of Marrakech. This mysterious city has gained a worldwide reputation thanks to the immense beauty & cultural diversity with which it has been endowed. Drive through the “Ville Nouvelle”, which was built by the French when Morocco was a French Protectorate from 1912 to 1956 to the botanical gardens of Majorelle. This haven of peace & tranquility was beautifully created by Jacques Majorelle who collected plants from all over the globe to come up with his dreamlike small paradise. Saint Laurent came later to add his unique artistic stamp offering, nowadays, the possibility to visit the Saint Laurent Museum that is located within the gardens.

Your cultural tour of Marrakech will take place from the Koutoubia Mosque (the landmark of Marrakech). The minaret covers the horizons of the pink city & its foundation goes back to the 12th century when the Almohad dynasty had conquered the Almoravid “Berber Warriors”. From the majestic Bab (gate) Agnaw, you’ll get to the Kasbah District to explore the Saadian Tombs. Then, meander around the intricate alleyways of the Mellah (Jewish Quarter) leading us to the Bahia Palace, which was built by the late Sultan Hassan I’s main vizier.

Stroll in the vibrant Souks that are divided into sections according to specialty before ending up the walking tour in the Jemaa El-Fna Square (the focal point of Marrakech). In the evening, it turns into a carnival filled with musicians, acrobats, snake-charmers, magicians, story-tellers, fortune-tellers and much more.

Day 3: Marrakech — High Atlas — Ait Benhaddou — Dades Valley

Today, you will head south crossing the high Atlas Mountainous chain that stands like a wall dividing Morocco’s south to its north. Drive through the spectacular Tizi n’Tichka Pass (2260 m), and then have a guided walking tour of the Ait Benhaddou Village. This UNESCO Listed village consists of numerous Kasbahs (fortresses) and has been used as a backdrop for shooting many Hollywood Movies including Lawrence of Arabia, Gladiator, Kingdom of Heaven, Prince of Persia and many others.

After lunch, we drive through Ouarzazate (Hollywood of Af Rica) with the possibility to include a visit to the famous Atlas Film Studios. Continue on the track further southeast through Skoura with its preserved Amredil Kasbah, then through the Valley of Roses before reaching our final destination of the day, the Dades Valley.

Day 4: Dades Valley — Todra Gorge — Erfoud — Merzouga

After having breakfast in your hotel, you’ll head to the Sahara Desert of Erg Chebbi while including a short break in the majestic Gorge of Todra (canyon) where you may want to stretch your legs while enjoying the stunning views. After lunch, you’ll drive through Erfoud is known for the dates production & fossil museums. When you arrive to the Erg Chebbi dunes, you’ll have a journey on a camel to attend the miraculous view of the sunset. Return back the desert camp for dinner followed by a time of entertainment.

Day 5: Merzouga — Ziz Valley — Midelt — Ifrane — Fez

Early wake up in the morning to attend the sunrise view atop the sand dune and after breakfast, you’ll continue on the track north to Fes through the palm-dotted Ziz Valley, Ziz Gorge, Midelt and Ifrane. You’ll include numerous stops for chilling out and for taking pictures. Lunch will take place in Midelt.

Day 6: Full-day Guided Tour of Fez

Today, you’ll spend the day exploring what Fes has to offer with your experienced tour guide. You’ll start with a drive uphill to enjoy the panoramic view of the Fes el-Bali (oldest Fes). Then, head to the Mellah (former Jewish Quarter) & Royal Palace where you can take pictures by the Golden Gates, and where you may want to stretch your legs in the vibrant street of the Mellah before getting inside the large old Medina of Fes.

Fes el-Bali is listed by the UNESCO as a World Heritage Site & is the largest car-free district in the world. The only means of transportation is mules and donkeys. The vibrant Souks and the bustling cafes along the historical monuments are what you can expect to come across.

Day 7: Fez — Chefchaouen

After having breakfast, you’ll head to Chefchaouen through the Rif Mountains. Upon arrival, you will embark on exploring this beautiful blue-village, which is nestled at the feet of the two mountains with the shape of goat’s horns after which Chefchaouen is named as in Tamazight language “Chaouen” means “Horns”.

The dominant aspect that has captivated the eyes, as well as the souls of visitors, is the blue color with which the whole town is painted. It was founded in the 15th century as a small fortress (Kasbah) to fight against the Portuguese invasion and then resided by the refugees who were expelled from Andalusia after the Reconquista. The blue was initially used by the Jewish people and then was adopted by everyone to the extent that no one is permitted, by law, to paint their building differently.

Meander around the intricate streets with its beautiful shutters and windows before reaching the natural spring of “Ras El-Ma) where you will see women washing clothes and carpets, and boys playing soccer in the nearby field. Chefchaouen is a live museum of art where the eye can never get bored; as a result, you may easily give up to getting lost in an exploration that nourishes the soul.

You may want to opt for doing some shopping (small items for souvenirs) as prices are reasonable and the vendors are free of hassle. Enjoy your lunch in one of the local restaurants overlooking the Kasbah and the famous Uta El-Hammam Square while watching the passers-by.

Day 8: Chefchaouen — Tangier

Today, you head to Tangier through the Rif Mountains for departure.

 Imperial Cities Tour 8 Days from Casablanca 
Overview

The Imperial Cities Tour 8 Days  from Casablanca offers an excellent opportunity to learn about the history and the heritage of Morocco. You’ll get acquainted with the dynasties that ruled this magical destination, the points of differences between all the aforementioned dynasties in terms of the architecture & lifestyle.

You’ll notice on the itinerary below that we have intentionally added the majestic Ouzoud Waterfalls so that you can enjoy the stunning views offered by this most beautiful waterfalls in Morocco. The other reason is to break the long drive from Fez to Marrakech.

You’ll have a professional and multilingual driver for this tour and local tour guides in Fez, Marrakech & Rabat. The local tour guides of the cities know the most hidden secrets of their native towns, and can share their knowledge and experiences with you.

Day 1: Casablanca — Rabat

Meet & greet upon arrival in the Mohammed V International Airport, then head to Rabat (if your arrival takes place in the morning, then we’ll have a guided tour of Rabat in the afternoon; otherwise, we’ll leave to next day).

Day 2: Explore Rabat — Meknes — Volubilis — Fez

After having breakfast in your hotel/Riad, you’ll have a guided tour of Rabat. This UNESCO listed as Heritage World Site since 2012 forms the first Imperial City to explore in our list. Rabat has served as the official and administrative capital of Morocco since 1912. It’s home of the King and the Royal Family, and where embassies, consulates, administrations, universities… are located.

Your guided tour of Rabat will begin in the dramatic Oudayas Kasbah, built by the edge of the everflowing Bou Regreg river and panoramically overlooking Sale and the Atlantic Ocean. The fortified walls were initially built by the Almoravids in the 11th century, then the Amohads destroyed the Kasbah and rebuilt it from scratch. The Kasbah is centered by a Mosque with its unique minaret. The typical Moroccan lifestyle is still maintained within the walls of this tranquil fortress including the local communal bakery, Hammam and a Mosque.

Have a walk through the Old Medina’s Souks to the Mohammed V Mausoleum and the adjacent Hassan Tower. Drive through the highway to Meknes, the second Imperial City in our list. You’ll begin your tour by the beautiful Bab Mansour Gate, the vibrant El Hedim Square, the Royal Granaries and stables before continuing on the trackto the Roman Ruins of Volubilis.

Explore the archaelogical site of Volubilis with a local guide before heading to Fez in the late afternoon.

Day 3: Full-Day Exploring Fez

Today, you’ll spend the day exploring the Fez Medina with a local tour guide. Drive uphill to get the panoramic view of Fez el-Bali (Oldest Fez), visit the Golden Gates of the Royal Palace and stretch your legs in the adjacent Mellah (former Jewish Quarter).

You’ll then have a guided walking tour of the Oldest Medina with its vibrant Souks, intricate alleyways and bustling cafes. You’ll visit the Nejjarine Museum, Chouara Tanneries, pass by the Al-Quarawyyine University and much more. The day tour Fez Medina will take back to medieval times being the largest urban car-free district in the world. In the late afternoon, your tour guide will escort you back to your accommodation.

Day 4: Fez — Azrou — Beni Mellal — Ouzoud Waterfalls

After having breakfast in your hotel/Riad, you’ll continue on the track through the Middle Atlas and the fertile plain near Beni Mellal, then head to the majestic Ouzoud Waterfalls. Enjoy the stunning views of the walls and your may want to stretch your legs underneath to get close to the fall. Spend the night in a guesthouse very close to the waterfall.

Day 5: Ouzoud Waterfalls — Marrakech

After breakfast, you may want to spend the morning in this spectacular waterfalls before heading to Marrakech in the afternoon. Drive through Tamlalt and when you arrive to Marrakech. We’ll escort you to your accommodation in the Medina or drop you off in your hotel in the Ville Nouvelle. In the evening, you may want to stretch your legs to the vibrant Square of Jemaa el-Fna.

Day 6: Full-day Exploring Marrakech

Today, you’ll spend the day exploring what Marrakech has to offer with a local tour guide. Visit the botanical gardens of Majorelle along with the Saint Laurent Museum. You’ll drive through the Ville Nouvelle that was built by the French when Morocco was a French Protectorate from 1912 to 1956. Start your walking tour by the Kotoubia Mosque (Landmark of Marrakech that can be seen from different directions). You’ll have the chance to learn about the rich history of Marrakech being a Moroccan Imperial City for centuries. Visit the Saadian Tombs that go back to the 15th century, then walk through the intricate alleyways of the Mellah with its conspicuous balconies.

You’ll visit the Bahia Palace that was built in the 19th century by the Sultan’s main vizier Ba Hmad. Explore the vibrant Souks of the Marrakech that are arranged into sections according to specialty to end up your tour in the Jemaa el-Fna, which is the focal point of Marrakech.

Day 7: Marrakech — Casablanca

After having breakfast in your hotel/Riad, you’ll have a free half-day in Marrakech to spend it on your own whether you want to go for some shopping, have a scrub to your entire body in a Hammam, or just stay in your hotel/Riad.

In the afternoon, you’ll drive to Casablanca where you’ll visit the majestic Hassan II Mosque before driving to your hotel in Casablanca’s city center.

Day 8: Depature

Today, it’s the time to say goodbye to Morocco with the hope you return in the future for further explorations in the amazing destination of Morocco. Transfer to the Mohammed V International Airp

Your guided tour of Rabat will begin in the dramatic Oudayas Kasbah, built by the edge of the everflowing Bou Regreg river and panoramically overlooking Sale and the Atlantic Ocean. The fortified walls were initially built by the Almoravids in the 11th century, then the Amohads destroyed the Kasbah and rebuilt it from scratch. The Kasbah is centered by a Mosque with its unique minaret. The typical Moroccan lifestyle is still maintained within the walls of this tranquil fortress including the local communal bakery, Hammam and a Mosque.

Have a walk through the Old Medina’s Souks to the Mohammed V Mausoleum and the adjacent Hassan Tower. Drive through the highway to Meknes, the second Imperial City in our list. You’ll begin your tour by the beautiful Bab Mansour Gate, the vibrant El Hedim Square, the Royal Granaries and stables before continuing on the trackto the Roman Ruins of Volubilis.

Explore the archaelogical site of Volubilis with a local guide before heading to Fez in the late afternoon.

Day 3: Full-Day Exploring Fez

Today, you’ll spend the day exploring the Fez Medina with a local tour guide. Drive uphill to get the panoramic view of Fez el-Bali (Oldest Fez), visit the Golden Gates of the Royal Palace and stretch your legs in the adjacent Mellah (former Jewish Quarter).

You’ll then have a guided walking tour of the Oldest Medina with its vibrant Souks, intricate alleyways and bustling cafes. You’ll visit the Nejjarine Museum, Chouara Tanneries, pass by the Al-Quarawyyine University and much more. The day tour Fez Medina will take back to medieval times being the largest urban car-free district in the world. In the late afternoon, your tour guide will escort you back to your accommodation.

Day 4: Fez — Azrou — Beni Mellal — Ouzoud Waterfalls

After having breakfast in your hotel/Riad, you’ll continue on the track through the Middle Atlas and the fertile plain near Beni Mellal, then head to the majestic Ouzoud Waterfalls. Enjoy the stunning views of the walls and your may want to stretch your legs underneath to get close to the fall. Spend the night in a guesthouse very close to the waterfall.

Day 5: Ouzoud Waterfalls — Marrakech

After breakfast, you may want to spend the morning in this spectacular waterfalls before heading to Marrakech in the afternoon. Drive through Tamlalt and when you arrive to Marrakech. We’ll escort you to your accommodation in the Medina or drop you off in your hotel in the Ville Nouvelle. In the evening, you may want to stretch your legs to the vibrant Square of Jemaa el-Fna.

Day 6: Full-day Exploring Marrakech

Today, you’ll spend the day exploring what Marrakech has to offer with a local tour guide. Visit the botanical gardens of Majorelle along with the Saint Laurent Museum. You’ll drive through the Ville Nouvelle that was built by the French when Morocco was a French Protectorate from 1912 to 1956. Start your walking tour by the Kotoubia Mosque (Landmark of Marrakech that can be seen from different directions). You’ll have the chance to learn about the rich history of Marrakech being a Moroccan Imperial City for centuries. Visit the Saadian Tombs that go back to the 15th century, then walk through the intricate alleyways of the Mellah with its conspicuous balconies.

You’ll visit the Bahia Palace that was built in the 19th century by the Sultan’s main vizier Ba Hmad. Explore the vibrant Souks of the Marrakech that are arranged into sections according to specialty to end up your tour in the Jemaa el-Fna, which is the focal point of Marrakech.

Day 7: Marrakech — Casablanca

After having breakfast in your hotel/Riad, you’ll have a free half-day in Marrakech to spend it on your own whether you want to go for some shopping, have a scrub to your entire body in a Hammam, or just stay in your hotel/Riad.

In the afternoon, you’ll drive to Casablanca where you’ll visit the majestic Hassan II Mosque before driving to your hotel in Casablanca’s city center.

Day 8: Depature

Today, it’s the time to say goodbye to Morocco with the hope you return in the future for further explorations in the amazing destination of Morocco. Transfer to the Mohammed V International Airp

Taste of the north: Chefchaouen, Assilah, Tetouan, Tangier / The North in 2 Days : Tangier, Tetouan ,Asilah, Chefchaouen
Overview

Taste of The North : Chaouen, Assilah ,Tetouan, Tangier by Xaeun Tours Discover enchanting Moroccan towns as you tour Asilah, Chefchaouen, and Tetouan. Relax with a private driver to escort you from town to town, soaking up many of Morocco’s highlights along the way.

Day 1: Tangier — Asilah — Chefchaouen

Meet your driver in the morning and head to Tetouan, a charming artistic and tranquil city. Stroll through the beautifully painted streets and visit the local harbor. Take a walk along the ramparts that were built by the Portuguese.

After exploring Tetouan, continue your journey to the blue city of Chefchaouen in the late afternoon. Check into your accommodation and then stroll through the blue-painted streets leading to the Utta el-Hamam Square.

Day 2: Chefchaouen — Tetouan — Tangier

After having breakfast at your hotel/riad, spend the first part of the day exploring the beautiful Medina of Chefchaouen with a local guide. Visit the kasbah that was founded by Moulay Ali Ibn Rashid, who used it as a base to fight against Portuguese invaders. Continue to the Ras el-Ma spring, where you can see local women washing clothes and rugs. Finish your tour at Utta el-Hamam Square and enjoy some mint tea while soaking in the local culture.

In the afternoon, head toward Asilah, a charming coastal town known for its vibrant murals and relaxed atmosphere. After exploring Asilah, return to Tangier for a 2-hour Kasbah and Medina walking tour. Drive through the Ville Nouvelle, which is divided into international districts that reflect the history of its diverse rulers during the International Zone period.

Highlights
Explore several of northern Morocco’s highlights on a privately escorted tour
Wander through the streets of the coastal, artistic town of Asilah
Discover the famous town of Chefchaouen and its iconic blue streets
Visit the Medina of Tetouan, a UNESCO World Heritage Site
Enjoy a walking tour to discover the highlights of the Tangier Medina and Kasbah

1 night accommodation with a breakfast in Chefchaouen at Villa Rita or similar

HomeToursFull Day trip to Asilah and Cap Spartel

 

Best Tangier to Asilah Day Trip
Overview

The full day tour from Tangier to Asilah day trip offers an excellente opportunity to spend the day exploring this little white jewel. Asilah has acquired popularity worldwide due to the immense beauty & cultural diversity with which it has been endowed. We’ll meet & greet you wherever you are, whether arriving at Tangier Ville Port  from Spain or pick up from your accommodation in Tangier .

Start from visiting the Hercules Cave, and the famous lighthouse of CAPSPARTEL, enjoy a camel ride and then get a VIP ride through the atlantic coast, to reach Asilah,

Once arriving to Asilah, you will have free time to visit and explore the white medina with it’s blue and green doors, Discover the main sights of the medina,

Afterwards, time to have lunch in a local restaurant, to try to mediterranean diet.

By 4Pm we take our way back to Tangier, Drop off in your accomodation and the end of our service.

What to expect

A Brief History

Modern-day Asilah dates back to the ancient Phoenician port of Zilis, which was founded around 1500 BC and located a few kilometers northeast of the present settlement. Much of Asilah’s medina was built under Arab Muslim rule between the 8th and 10th centuries AD. The dramatic ramparts that surround it, however, were built by the Portuguese, who conquered the town in 1471. The Portuguese maintained control of Asilah until 1589, when it briefly returned to Moroccan rule before being taken over by the Spanish.

In the 17th century Moroccans won control of the town again and in the 18th and 19th centuries it became a notorious base for pirates. This led to widespread bombardment by the Austrians in 1829 and from 1912 to 1956, Asilah was once more part of Spanish Morocco. Today it has regained its independence but its colonial past is reflected in the distinctly Iberian flavor of its cuisine and culture.

Start your adventure with a convenient pick-up from your hotel in Tangier. Begin your journey to Tetouan, a UNESCO World Heritage site, with breathtaking views of the Rif Mountains along the way.

Upon arrival, you’ll have the freedom to explore the enchanting medina at your own pace. Wander through its narrow, winding streets lined with traditional Moroccan architecture, vibrant markets, and artisan shops. From Tangier: Chefchaouen Day Trip with traditional lunch

Join us on a day trip from Tangier to Chefchaouen, with hotel pickup and drop-off included. Explore the history of Chefchaouen, the main square, and its charming blue streets.

Starting at 9:00 AM, travel into the Rif Mountains to discover the beautiful blue doors and white-walled Medina, originally a Berber post.

Once you arrive, enjoy a delicious traditional Moroccan lunch. Visit the main square, where you’ll find the 18th-century kasbah, central mosque, and cafes offering authentic Moroccan cuisine. Stroll through the market to find a mix of traditional Moroccan and tourist souvenirs.

Don’t miss the Jewish Quarter behind the jewelry souk for a unique glimpse into the city’s heritage. Afterward, drive back to Tangier to catch the sunset with a panoramic view, followed by drop-off around 7:00 PM.

Visit the beautiful old town of Chefchaouen
Discover the history and the culture of Chefchaouen

Desert Tour from  Tangier 

This private 5 days tour from Tangier is a combination between the beauty of Morocco and the magical Sahara of Merzouga,

Starting from Tangier, Drive through the rif Mountains to Reach the blue Pearl of Chefchaouen, Explore the blue streets and the tiny doors. with an introduction to the local culture, then go to Fes – the heart of the moroccan culture- . Spending a night there and drive to the Sahara, Our main attraction there you’ll have a camel ride and an optional buggy or quad ride. See the sunset/sunrise up the dunes, Spend the night in a local desert camp. Then we’ll cross the atlas mountains passing by Todgha Gorges, Dades Valey, Skoura, Ouerzazate, AitbenHadou Until we end our tour in Marrakech -the pearl of the south

Day 1: Tangier – Chefchaouen

Meet and greet with our driver. And start the journey to the blue pearl

We will drive through the captivating views of the Rif Mountains towards the beautiful city of Tetouan and charming little villages.

Once we get to the Blue Chefchaouen, we will first visit the captivating Ras El Ma, the most beautiful river, where the water comes gushing out of the mountain.

Next, you’ll be enjoying the calm-washed streets of the Medina, enjoying its calm, and relaxing atmosphere. You would also get to see the most captivating Moroccan handicrafts. The city has also an authentic architecture influenced by the old Jewish culture.

After a meal for dinner, we will spend the night in a local Riad / Hotel in the blue city

Accommodation included: Accommodation 4stars

Day 2:  Chefchaouen – Fes

We will take you through the fabulous views of the Rif Mountains, crossing Meknes, the magnificent 17th century old capital.

Next, we will pass by Moulay Idriss Zerhoun, the holiest town in Morocco, and then Volubilis, the ancient Roman Ruins.

The last stop on today ‘s Tangier to Marrakech Desert Tours, would be Fes, the oldest city in Morocco, another world of narrow streets and medieval Medinas.

We will enjoy a traditional Fessi dinner, and spend the night in a Moroccan Moroccan Style Riad.

Meals included: Breakfast
Accommodation included: Accommodation IN At Riad 4Stars

Day 3: Fes – Ifrane – Sahara Desert 

On the next morning of the Tangier to Marrakech Sahara Tours, we will wake up to the spectacular views of the Middle Atlas Mountains.

Our first destination would be Immouzer, the lovely Berber village with the most beautiful views of green landscapes and flowing rivers.

After that, we will continue our way through the green Cedar Forests to Ifrane, “The Moroccan Little Switzerland”. Our team will take you through the French-style city, enjoying its cool weather and fascinating architecture.

Next, we will have a break in Azrou, to learn more about the ancient Berber culture and the magnificent Cedar forest, to see the oldest tree in Morocco and to have some fun with the little tricky monkeys. We will continue to Midlet, the Berber village of Rocks, to taste a typical Berber Tagine for lunch, and know more about the ancient Berber culture.

Last, we will continue towards Merzouga, the Golden Gate of the Sahara, where we will have a spectacular trek admiring the magical sunrise and the sensational tranquility of the majestic Sahara.

Our camels would take us to our exotic  Desert Camp in the Middle of the Curving Dunes, to gather around the campfire, enjoy a Berber Folklore show, and stare the captivating starry night sky.

We will enjoy a traditional Berber meal for dinner, then spend the night in Luxury Desert Camp

Meals included:
• Breakfast
• Dinner
Accommodation included: Luxury Desert Camp

Day 4: Desert Erg Chebbi – Todra Gorges – Dades Valley

The next morning is beautiful, we will wake up early in the morning, and we will see you soon. After that, we will be back to our hotel in Merzouga, to have breakfast and a warm relaxing shower.

We will leave Merzouga towards Rissani, the home of the Moroccan Ruling dynasty, we would like to introduce you to its colorful Souks and ancient culture.

We will move towards Todra Gorges, to make the most of the amazing orange cliffs, making a perfect destination for climbers and adventure lovers. We would also take you through its authentic mosques, colorful lanterns, and famous textiles.

Later on, we continue our Sahara Tours to Dades Valley, the little green paradise in the bare Sahara. We will have our local dinner, then spend the night in a little Guesthouse across the river.
Duration: 40 minutes

Meals included:
• Breakfast
• Dinner

Accommodation included:

Accommodation in the hotel 4stars

Day 5:  Dades Valley – Skoura – Ouarzazate – Ait Ben Haddou – Marrakech

The last day of our tour, we will have a warm  breakfast, then leave the beautiful valley towards Ouarzazate, passing by Skoura, and its magnificent Kasbah of Amridil and enjoy a tempting Local lunch.

After that, we will explore Ouarzazate walking through the colorful old Souks and authentic ancient mud streets. Our team will take you through the magnificent International Film Studio, to be part of the greatest Hollywood movies and Series were made as Game of Thrones, Gladiator, Babel, The Jewel of the Nile.

We continue our Sahara Tours to the Kasbah of Ait Ben Haddou, discovering ancient Royal Palaces, as you get amazed by real authentic Moroccan architecture. We will have a delicious lunch in a traditional Berber restaurant, to taste the most delicious combination of Moroccan flavors. Then drive through the winding roads of Tizi N Tichka Pass.

The last destination on your Tangier to Marrakesh Desert Tours, would the old Red Marrakesh, where we will leave you to find a new Moroccan culture of friendly locals and old traditions.and the end of our service

We will enjoy a traditional Fessi dinner, and spend the night in a Moroccan Moroccan Style Riad.

Meals included: Breakfast

Accommodation included: Accommodation IN At Riad 4Stars

Day 3: Fes – Ifrane – Sahara Desert

On the next morning of the Tangier to Marrakech Sahara Tours, we will wake up to the spectacular views of the Middle Atlas Mountains.

Our first destination would be Immouzer, the lovely Berber village with the most beautiful views of green landscapes and flowing rivers.

After that, we will continue our way through the green Cedar Forests to Ifrane, “The Moroccan Little Switzerland”. Our team will take you through the French-style city, enjoying its cool weather and fascinating architecture.

Next, we will have a break in Azrou, to learn more about the ancient Berber culture and the magnificent Cedar forest, to see the oldest tree in Morocco and to have some fun with the little tricky monkeys. We will continue to Midlet, the Berber village of Rocks, to taste a typical Berber Tagine for lunch, and know more about the ancient Berber culture.

Last, we will continue towards Merzouga, the Golden Gate of the Sahara, where we will have a spectacular trek admiring the magical sunrise and the sensational tranquility of the majestic Sahara.

Our camels would take us to our exotic Desert Camp in the Middle of the Curving Dunes, to gather around the campfire, enjoy a Berber Folklore show, and stare the captivating starry night sky.

We will enjoy a traditional Berber meal for dinner, then spend the night in Luxury Desert Camp

Meals included:

• Breakfast

• Dinner

Accommodation included: Luxury Desert Camp

Day 4: Desert Erg Chebbi – Todra Gorges – Dades Valley

The next morning is beautiful, we will wake up early in the morning, and we will see you soon. After that, we will be back to our hotel in Merzouga, to have breakfast and a warm relaxing shower.

We will leave Merzouga towards Rissani, the home of the Moroccan Ruling dynasty, we would like to introduce you to its colorful Souks and ancient culture.

We will move towards Todra Gorges, to make the most of the amazing orange cliffs, making a perfect destination for climbers and adventure lovers. We would also take you through its authentic mosques, colorful lanterns, and famous textiles.

Later on, we continue our Sahara Tours to Dades Valley, the little green paradise in the bare Sahara. We will have our local dinner, then spend the night in a little Guesthouse across the river.

Duration: 40 minutes

Meals included:

• Breakfast

• Dinner

Accommodation included:

Accommodation in the hotel 4stars

Day 5: Dades Valley – Skoura – Ouarzazate – Ait Ben Haddou – Marrakech

The last day of our tour, we will have a warm breakfast, then leave the beautiful valley towards Ouarzazate, passing by Skoura, and its magnificent Kasbah of Amridil and enjoy a tempting Local lunch.

After that, we will explore Ouarzazate walking through the colorful old Souks and authentic ancient mud streets. Our team will take you through the magnificent International Film Studio, to be part of the greatest Hollywood movies and Series were made as Game of Thrones, Gladiator, Babel, The Jewel of the Nile.

We continue our Sahara Tours to the Kasbah of Ait Ben Haddou, discovering ancient Royal Palaces, as you get amazed by real authentic Moroccan architecture. We will have a delicious lunch in a traditional Berber restaurant, to taste the most delicious combination of Moroccan flavors. Then drive through the winding roads of Tizi N Tichka Pass.

The last destination on your Tangier to Marrakesh Desert Tours, would the old Red Marrakesh, where we will leave you to find a new Moroccan culture of friendly locals and old traditions.and the end of our service

The North of Morocco in 3 Days  Including Fes 

Discover enchanting Moroccan towns as you tour Tangier, Asilah, Chefchaouen, and Tetouan ; In Addition to the spiritual capital of Morocco , Fes.

Discover several of Morocco’s main highlights on a 3 day trip from Tangier. Visit the charming towns of Asilah, Chefchaouen, Tetouan and the magical Fes ; on a tour with a private multilingual driver.

Day 1: Tangier — Tetouan — Chefchaouen

Meet and greet with your driver in the morning wether you are arriving by ferry or plane. Start your journey by heading to Tetouan, the UNESCO-listed Medina, have leisure time to discover the city through your own prespective.

Continue to the blue city of Chefchaouen in the afternoon. Make your way to your accommodation for check in and have a guided tour with your local guide to discover the history and enjoy the bleu painted streets.

Day 2:  Chefchaouen — Fes

After having breakfast at your hotel/riad. Take the road towards the spiritual capital of Morocco ; Get ready to take to the road towards the captivating city of Fes. Check in your hotel/riad ; Have a full guided tour in one of the cities that are full and rich with history

Day 3: Fes — Asilah — Tangier 

Today’s program will start by heading towards Asilah ; A charming artistic and tranquil city. Stroll through the beautifully painted white streets and visit the local harbor. Take a walk along the ramparts that were built by the Portuguese.

After that back in Tangier, head to the promontory Cap Spartel to enjoy views of the Mediterranean Sea and the Atlantic Ocean. Enjoy an optional camel ride experience by the sandy Atlantic beach before visiting the Neolithic Caves of Hercules.

Also you will enjoy a 2-hour Kasbah and Medina walking tour in Tangier. Drive through the Ville Nouvelle, which is divided into international districts according to the countries that took part in its rule when the city was an International Zone.

From Tangier: Northern Morocco 3-Day Tour with Accommodation

Embark on an immersive journey through Morocco’s enchanting Rif region with our tour from Tangier. This adventure showcases the vibrant culture, stunning landscapes, and rich history of three remarkable towns: Assilah, Tetouan, and Chefchaouen.

Day 1: Tangier to Assilah
Begin your journey in Tangier with a panoramic visit of the city, soaking in its unique blend of cultures and coastal charm. Afterward, travel to Assilah, known for its beautiful beaches and artistic murals. Enjoy a free afternoon to explore the town’s quaint streets, local galleries, and vibrant markets. Return to Tangier for the night.

Day 2: Tangier to Tetouan and Chefchaouen
After breakfast, set off to Tetouan, a UNESCO World Heritage site famous for its well-preserved medina and rich Andalusian influence. Discover the town’s unique architecture and local crafts. Later, head to Chefchaouen, the “Blue City,” renowned for its striking blue-washed buildings and stunning mountain backdrop. Enjoy a free afternoon to wander its charming streets and relax in a café.

Day 3: Chefchaouen to Tangier
After breakfast, take some time to soak in the beauty of Chefchaouen before returning to Tangier. Reflect on your memorable experiences in this captivating region, rich in culture and natural beauty.

This tour is perfect for adventure seekers and culture enthusiasts alike, offering a unique glimpse into the heart of Northern Morocco. Join us for an unforgettable experience!

Highlights:

Enjoy stunning views of this vibrant coastal city.
Discover the UNESCO-listed medina and Andalusian architecture.
Wander through the iconic blue-washed streets.
Marvel at the scenic beauty of the Rif region.
Enjoy a mix of guided tours and free exploration.

From Tangier: 4 Days Northern Morocco Tour including Fes and Volubilis
Day 1: Tangier – Asilah – Meknes – Volubilis – Fes

Your journey begins in Tangier, a dynamic gateway between Europe and Africa, known for its rich cultural blend and coastal beauty. After meeting your driver, enjoy a panoramic tour of the city, including a visit to Cap Spartel, where the Atlantic Ocean meets the Mediterranean Sea, followed by a stop at the famous Hercules Caves. You may also enjoy an optional camel ride along the Atlantic coastline.

Continue to Asilah, a charming seaside town known for its whitewashed medina, artistic murals, and relaxed atmosphere, where you will enjoy a guided visit. Then travel onward to Meknes, one of Morocco’s imperial cities, known for its rich heritage and impressive monuments.

Afterwards, visit the ancient Roman site of Volubilis with a local guide, where you will explore well-preserved mosaics and historical ruins.

In the afternoon, continue to Fes, where you will check in to your riad for an overnight stay.

Day 2: Fes Guided Tour

After breakfast, meet your local guide to explore Fes, widely regarded as the spiritual and cultural heart of Morocco. Your guided tour will take you through the historic medina of Fes el-Bali, a UNESCO World Heritage Site renowned for its maze of narrow streets, vibrant souks, traditional tanneries, and historic religious schools. This immersive experience offers a deep insight into the city’s rich history, architecture, and living traditions. At the end of the day, return to your riad and enjoy a relaxing evening.

Day 3: Fes – Chefchaouen

Following breakfast, depart for Chefchaouen, the famous “Blue City” nestled in the Rif Mountains. Upon arrival, meet your local guide and enjoy a walking tour through its picturesque medina, known for its striking blue-painted streets, charming alleyways, and unique artisan shops. The tranquil atmosphere and scenic beauty of Chefchaouen make it one of Morocco’s most captivating destinations. After the visit, check in to your riad or hotel and enjoy an overnight stay.

Day 4: Chefchaouen – Tetouan – Tangier

After breakfast, depart towards Tetouan, a UNESCO-listed city distinguished by its strong Andalusian heritage and one of the most authentic medinas in Morocco. Enjoy a guided tour through its historic streets, lively markets, and cultural landmarks, discovering its unique blend of Moroccan and Spanish influences. After the visit, return to Tangier, where you will be dropped off at the airport, port, or your accommodation, marking the end of your memorable journey.

 

Accommodation

2 nights in Fes  with breakfast                       Dar Nokhba inn & Villa Rita Or  similar
1 night in Chefchaouen with breakfast      Riad Tafilalet Or similar

From Tangier Day Trip To Paradise Beach
 

Explore Morocco’s countryside on a self-guided Tangier day trip. See ancient landscapes and cities, ride on a camel, and enjoy lunch before heading for a swim at Paradise Beach Briech.

Take a relaxing day trip to Paradise Beach Briech, with pickup and drop-off included. Enjoy a camel ride, sightseeing, lunch, and free time to take in one of the best beaches in Morocco.

First, begin with pickup from your hotel. Enjoy a panoramic tour of Tangier, where you’ll see Perdicaris Parc, Cape Spartel, and Hercules Caves. Stop for a camel ride before continuing south.

Then, arrive at Briech. Take a break with lunch served by locals before continuing to the small town of Assilah. Next, continue to Paradise Beach Briech. Take time to swim and enjoy the Atlantic.

Finally, relax on the way back to your drop-off area on the return to Tangier. Arrive back in time to enjoy your night out in the city.

Finally, relax on the way back to your drop-off area on the return to Tangier. Arrive back in time to enjoy your night out in the city.

From Tangier Full Day Trip to Chefchaouen and Akchour Waterfalls

 

From Tangier Full Day Trip to Chefchaouen and Akchour Waterfalls. Enjoy a day trip to Chefchaouen, a beautiful blue door and white-walled medina that was originally a Berber post. See the main square, souks, the Jewish quarter, and Akchour waterfalls in the area.

Visit Chefchaouen, a beautiful blue door and white-walled medina that was originally a Berber post. The main square is the heart of Chefchaouen, with the 18th-century kasbah, a central mosque, and cafes with authentic Moroccan-style menus.

The market is a mix of traditional Moroccan and tourist souvenirs – it’s often very busy, but worth a visit for the bargains.

On your walking tour with your guide, explore the kasbah which was built by Moulay Ismail and is now practically a ruin next to the Great Mosque. Visit the charming main square and the souks that sell an array of goods.

If you head to the Jewish quarter behind the jewelry souk, you will feel the difference between it and the main Arabic feel of the town.

Afterward, a small hike to discover the small waterfalls of Akchour, a natural beauty . A local tour guide is provided at Akchour.

Highlights
Explore the tiny streets, the blue doors and the colorful stairs
Take a panoramic views of the city from the hills
Walk into the national park, discover the beauty of local nature

From Tangier Day Trip to Tetouan

Embark on a cultural day-trip to Tetouan from Tangier with a multilingual professional driver. You’ll spend the day out exploring the UNESCO listed Old Medina of Tetouan with its deep-seated cultural heritage that has been preserved and passed on from one generation to another. You’ll have a guided walking tour through the bustling lanes of the Medina that are shrouded in mystery. The Medina remains intact compared to other Moroccan counterparts with its Andalusian Architecture intermingled with the Moroccan touch creating an amalgam of myriad cultures. Visit the Museum of Archaeology, the vibrant Souks, the Jewish & the Spanish Quarters and much more. In the late afternoon, you’ll drive back to Tangier.

Highlights:

Embark on a full day trip to Tetouan from Tangier
Visit the Medina of Tetouan, a UNESCO World Heritage Site
Benefit from pickup and drop-off at various points in Tangier
King Palace Square
Old Jewish quarter of Mellah

From Tangier Day Trip to Tetouan

Scenic Journey Through the Rif Mountains:

Your adventure begins with a comfortable drive through the stunning Rif Mountains, showcasing rugged peaks, lush valleys, and charming villages. Stop at a panoramic viewpoint to capture breathtaking photos and soak in the serene beauty of northern Morocco.

Explore Chefchaouen’s Iconic Medina:

Upon arrival, meet your local guide for a captivating walking tour of Chefchaouen’s Medina. Stroll through its famous blue-painted streets, vibrant souks, and landmarks like the Kasbah Museum and the Grand Mosque. Discover the unique Andalusian-inspired architecture and learn about the rich history and culture that make this town a must-visit.

Savor Authentic Moroccan Flavors:

Enjoy a delightful Moroccan lunch at a traditional restaurant, featuring local delicacies such as fragrant tagines, fluffy couscous, and freshly baked khobz, all complemented by refreshing Moroccan mint tea. The warm hospitality and authentic flavors make this dining experience unforgettable. Leisure Time in Chefchaouen:

After lunch, take some leisure time to explore at your own pace. Shop for unique handcrafted souvenirs, capture postcard-worthy photos, or relax in one of the peaceful squares, immersing yourself in the town’s tranquil atmosphere.

Unwind with a Camel Ride on the Atlantic Coast: On your return to Tangier, add excitement with a memorable camel ride and a visit to Cap Spartel along the Atlantic coast. Feel the sea breeze as you traverse the golden sands, providing a perfect finale to your day and a refreshing contrast to Chefchaouen’s mountainous charm.

Highlights:

Discover the enchanting “Blue Pearl” of Morocco on a private day trip.
Enjoy a scenic drive through the stunning Rif Mountains to Chefchaouen.
Explore the Medina with a local guide and uncover the town’s rich history.
Experience a camel ride along the Atlantic coast.
Enjoy the flexibility of a private tour with a dedicated driver.

Private transportation in a modern, air-conditioned vehicle

Free time for personal exploration

Camel ride by the Atlantic coast
cap spartel visit
